title: Area Charts
text: Area charts line charts area line filled. ideal showing values change time emphasizing magnitude change. Area charts can stacked show composition whole.

title: Area Charts
code: library(muiCharts) library(dplyr) library(tidyr)

title: Area Charts
text: ’ll use starwars dataset create various area chart examples:
code: # Count characters by gender across films starwars_gender <- dplyr::starwars |>   unnest_longer(films) |>   count(films, sex) |>   filter(!is.na(sex)) |>   pivot_wider(names_from = sex, values_from = n, values_fill = 0) |>   mutate(release = c(1977, 2002, 1983, 2005, 1980, 2015, 1999)) |>   arrange(release)  # Character counts by birth year (aggregated into bins) starwars_birth <- dplyr::starwars |>   filter(!is.na(birth_year)) |>   mutate(birth_decade = floor(birth_year / 10) * 10) |>   count(birth_decade) |>   arrange(birth_decade)

title: Area Charts
text: simple area chart showing character counts:
code: LineChart(   dataset = starwars_gender,   xAxis = list(     list(       scaleType = "point",       dataKey = "films",       label = "Movies",       tickLabelStyle = list(         fontSize = "0.5em"       )     )   ),   series = list(     list(dataKey = "male", label = "Male", area = TRUE, showMark = FALSE)   ),   height = 300 )

title: Area Charts
text: Display multiple areas comparison:
code: LineChart(   dataset = starwars_gender,   xAxis = list(     list(       scaleType = "point",       dataKey = "films",       label = "Movies",       tickLabelStyle = list(         fontSize = "0.5em"       )     )   ),   series = list(     list(dataKey = "male", label = "Male", area = TRUE, showMark = FALSE),     list(dataKey = "female", label = "Female", area = TRUE, showMark = FALSE)   ),   height = 300 )

title: Area Charts
text: Stack areas top show composition:
code: LineChart(   dataset = starwars_gender,   xAxis = list(     list(       scaleType = "point",       dataKey = "films",       label = "Movies",       tickLabelStyle = list(         fontSize = "0.5em"       )     )   ),   series = list(     list(dataKey = "male", label = "Male", stack = "total", area = TRUE, showMark = FALSE),     list(dataKey = "female", label = "Female", stack = "total", area = TRUE, showMark = FALSE),     list(dataKey = "hermaphroditic", label = "Hermaphroditic", stack = "total", area = TRUE, showMark = FALSE),     list(dataKey = "none", label = "None", stack = "total", area = TRUE, showMark = FALSE)   ),   height = 300 )

title: Area Charts
text: Show data points area chart:
code: LineChart(   dataset = starwars_birth,   xAxis = list(     list(       scaleType = "linear",       dataKey = "birth_decade",       label = "Birth Decade (BBY)"     )   ),   series = list(     list(dataKey = "n", label = "Characters Born", area = TRUE, showMark = TRUE)   ),   height = 300 )

title: Area Charts
text: Customize area curve using different interpolation methods:
code: LineChart(   dataset = starwars_gender,   xAxis = list(     list(       scaleType = "point",       dataKey = "films",       label = "Movies",       tickLabelStyle = list(         fontSize = "0.5em"       )     )   ),   series = list(     list(dataKey = "male", label = "Male", area = TRUE, showMark = FALSE, curve = "natural")   ),   height = 300 )

title: Area Charts
text: Show relative proportions time:
code: LineChart(   dataset = starwars_gender,   xAxis = list(     list(       scaleType = "point",       dataKey = "films",       label = "Movies",       tickLabelStyle = list(         fontSize = "0.5em"       )     )   ),   series = list(     list(dataKey = "male", label = "Male", stack = "total", area = TRUE, showMark = FALSE),     list(dataKey = "female", label = "Female", stack = "total", area = TRUE, showMark = FALSE),     list(dataKey = "hermaphroditic", label = "Hermaphroditic", stack = "total", area = TRUE, showMark = FALSE),     list(dataKey = "none", label = "None", stack = "total", area = TRUE, showMark = FALSE)   ),   stackOffset = "expand",   height = 300 )

title: Area Charts
text: Customize baseline area fill:
code: # Create data with negative values starwars_variation <- starwars_gender |>   mutate(     male_diff = male - lag(male, default = male[1]),     female_diff = female - lag(female, default = female[1])   )  LineChart(   dataset = starwars_variation,   xAxis = list(     list(       scaleType = "point",       dataKey = "films",       label = "Movies",       tickLabelStyle = list(         fontSize = "0.5em"       )     )   ),   series = list(     list(dataKey = "male_diff", label = "Male (Change)", area = TRUE, showMark = FALSE, baseline = "min")   ),   height = 300 )

title: Area Charts
text: Handle missing data gracefully:
code: # Create data with missing values starwars_incomplete <- starwars_gender |>   mutate(     male_partial = ifelse(row_number() %in% c(3, 5), NA, male)   )  LineChart(   dataset = starwars_incomplete,   xAxis = list(     list(       scaleType = "point",       dataKey = "films",       label = "Movies",       tickLabelStyle = list(         fontSize = "0.5em"       )     )   ),   series = list(     list(dataKey = "male_partial", label = "Male (with gaps)", area = TRUE, showMark = TRUE, connectNulls = FALSE)   ),   height = 300 )

title: Area Charts
text: Connect areas across null values:
code: LineChart(   dataset = starwars_incomplete,   xAxis = list(     list(       scaleType = "point",       dataKey = "films",       label = "Movies",       tickLabelStyle = list(         fontSize = "0.5em"       )     )   ),   series = list(     list(dataKey = "male_partial", label = "Male (connected)", area = TRUE, showMark = TRUE, connectNulls = TRUE)   ),   height = 300 )

title: Area Charts
text: Use numeric values x-axis:
code: LineChart(   dataset = starwars_gender,   xAxis = list(     list(       scaleType = "linear",       dataKey = "release",       label = "Release Year"     )   ),   series = list(     list(dataKey = "male", label = "Male", stack = "total", area = TRUE, showMark = FALSE),     list(dataKey = "female", label = "Female", stack = "total", area = TRUE, showMark = FALSE)   ),   grid = list(horizontal = TRUE),   height = 300 )

title: Area Charts
text: AreaElement AnimatedArea sub-components rendered AreaPlot filled area animation. Call explore props: Use slotProps AreaPlot configure components:
code: AreaElement(skipAnimation = TRUE) AnimatedArea(skipAnimation = TRUE) ChartDataProvider(   dataset = starwars_gender,   height = 300,   xAxis = list(     list(       scaleType = "point",       dataKey = "films",       tickLabelStyle = list(fontSize = "0.5em")     )   ),   series = list(     list(type = "line", dataKey = "male", label = "Male", area = TRUE),     list(type = "line", dataKey = "female", label = "Female", area = TRUE)   ),   ChartsTooltip(),   ChartsSurface(     AreaPlot(slotProps = list(area = list(skipAnimation = TRUE))),     LinePlot(),     ChartsXAxis(),     ChartsYAxis()   ) )

title: Area Charts
text: MUI Area Charts Documentation See also: Tooltip value formatters, Styling colors, Composition advanced layout, Axis & Grid grid axis options.

title: Axis & Grid
text: scaleType property defines data maps axis positions. Available types: "band" — categorical (bar charts) "point" — categorical (line/scatter charts) "linear" — numeric (default value axes) "log" — logarithmic "sqrt" — square root "time" — date/time values

title: Axis & Grid
code: starwars_gender <- dplyr::starwars |>   unnest_longer(films) |>   count(films, sex) |>   filter(!is.na(sex)) |>   pivot_wider(names_from = sex, values_from = n) |>   mutate(release = c(1977, 2002, 1983, 2005, 1980, 2015, 1999)) |>   arrange(release)  BarChart(   dataset = starwars_gender,   xAxis = list(     list(scaleType = "band", dataKey = "films")   ),   series = list(list(dataKey = "male", label = "Male")),   height = 300 )

title: Axis & Grid
code: LineChart(   dataset = data.frame(x = c(1, 2, 5, 10, 20), y = c(2, 5, 10, 15, 30)),   xAxis = list(list(scaleType = "linear", dataKey = "x")),   series = list(list(dataKey = "y")),   height = 300 )

title: Axis & Grid
code: LineChart(   dataset = data.frame(x = c(1, 10, 100, 1000), y = c(1, 2, 3, 4)),   xAxis = list(list(scaleType = "log", dataKey = "x")),   series = list(list(dataKey = "y")),   height = 300 )

title: Axis & Grid
text: Add labels axes using label property:
code: BarChart(   dataset = starwars_gender,   xAxis = list(     list(       scaleType = "band",       dataKey = "films",       label = "Star Wars Films",       tickLabelStyle = list(fontSize = "0.5em")     )   ),   yAxis = list(     list(label = "Number of Characters")   ),   series = list(list(dataKey = "male", label = "Male")),   height = 300 )

title: Axis & Grid
text: Customize axis values displayed using valueFormatter:
code: BarChart(   dataset = starwars_gender,   xAxis = list(     list(       scaleType = "band",       dataKey = "films",       tickLabelStyle = list(fontSize = "0.5em")     )   ),   yAxis = list(     list(       valueFormatter = JS("(value) => `${value} characters`"),       width = 100     )   ),   series = list(list(dataKey = "male", label = "Male")),   height = 300 )

title: Axis & Grid
text: Customize tick label appearance tickLabelStyle:
code: BarChart(   dataset = starwars_gender,   xAxis = list(     list(       scaleType = "band",       dataKey = "films",       height = 100,       tickLabelStyle = list(         fontSize = "0.6em",         angle = 45       )     )   ),   series = list(list(dataKey = "male", label = "Male")),   height = 350 )

title: Axis & Grid
text: Use multiple y-axes series different scales:
code: starwars_multi <- starwars |>   filter(!is.na(height), !is.na(mass), !is.na(birth_year), mass < 200) |>   select(name, height, mass, birth_year) |>   head(10)  LineChart(   dataset = starwars_multi,   xAxis = list(     list(scaleType = "point", dataKey = "name", tickLabelStyle = list(fontSize = "0.5em"))   ),   yAxis = list(     list(id = "leftAxis", label = "Height (cm)"),     list(id = "rightAxis", label = "Mass (kg)")   ),   series = list(     list(dataKey = "height", label = "Height", yAxisId = "leftAxis"),     list(dataKey = "mass", label = "Mass", yAxisId = "rightAxis")   ),   height = 350 )

title: Axis & Grid
text: Add grid lines grid prop:
code: BarChart(   dataset = starwars_gender,   xAxis = list(     list(scaleType = "band", dataKey = "films", tickLabelStyle = list(fontSize = "0.5em"))   ),   series = list(     list(dataKey = "male", label = "Male"),     list(dataKey = "female", label = "Female")   ),   grid = list(horizontal = TRUE, vertical = TRUE),   height = 300 )

title: Axis & Grid
text: Use ChartsReferenceLine add horizontal vertical reference lines. requires composition API:
code: library(muiMaterial) #>  #> Attaching package: 'muiMaterial' #> The following object is masked from 'package:muiCharts': #>  #>     Toolbar  avg_male <- round(mean(starwars_gender$male, na.rm = TRUE))  ChartDataProvider(   dataset = starwars_gender,   height = 350,   xAxis = list(     list(       scaleType = "band",       dataKey = "films",       tickLabelStyle = list(fontSize = "0.5em")     )   ),   series = list(     list(type = "bar", dataKey = "male", label = "Male")   ),   ChartsTooltip(),   ChartsSurface(     ChartsGrid(horizontal = TRUE),     BarPlot(),     ChartsReferenceLine(       y = avg_male,       label = paste("Avg:", avg_male),       lineStyle = list(stroke = "red", strokeDasharray = "5 5")     ),     ChartsXAxis(),     ChartsYAxis()   ) )

title: Axis & Grid
text: Control spacing SVG edge drawing area margin prop:
code: BarChart(   dataset = starwars_gender,   xAxis = list(     list(scaleType = "band", dataKey = "films", tickLabelStyle = list(fontSize = "0.5em"))   ),   series = list(list(dataKey = "male", label = "Male")),   margin = list(top = 20, bottom = 60, left = 80, right = 20),   height = 300 )

title: Axis & Grid
text: details axis customization, visit MUI X Axis documentation.

title: Bar Charts
text: Bar charts express quantities bar’s length, using common baseline. ideal comparing discrete categories excel visualizing differences magnitude across categories, highlighting trends, comparing proportions glance.

title: Bar Charts
code: library(muiCharts) library(dplyr) library(tidyr)

title: Bar Charts
text: ’ll use starwars dataset dplyr examples. Let’s prepare data counting characters species homeworld:
code: # Count characters by species starwars_species <- dplyr::starwars |>   count(species) |>   filter(!is.na(species)) |>   arrange(desc(n)) |>   slice_head(n = 7)  # Count characters by homeworld starwars_homeworld <- dplyr::starwars |>   count(homeworld) |>   filter(!is.na(homeworld)) |>   arrange(desc(n)) |>   slice_head(n = 8)  # Count characters by gender across films starwars_gender <- dplyr::starwars |>   unnest_longer(films) |>   count(films, sex) |>   filter(!is.na(sex)) |>   pivot_wider(names_from = sex, values_from = n, values_fill = 0) |>   mutate(release = c(1977, 2002, 1983, 2005, 1980, 2015, 1999)) |>   arrange(release)

title: Bar Charts
text: simple vertical bar chart showing count characters species:
code: BarChart(   dataset = starwars_species,   xAxis = list(     list(       scaleType = "band",       dataKey = "species",       label = "Species"     )   ),   series = list(     list(dataKey = "n", label = "Count")   ),   height = 300 )

title: Bar Charts
text: can create horizontal bars using layout = "horizontal" property:
code: BarChart(   dataset = starwars_homeworld,   layout = "horizontal",   yAxis = list(     list(       scaleType = "band",       dataKey = "homeworld",       label = "Homeworld"     )   ),   series = list(     list(dataKey = "n", label = "Count")   ),   height = 350 )

title: Bar Charts
text: Display multiple series side side:
code: BarChart(   dataset = starwars_gender,   xAxis = list(     list(       scaleType = "band",       dataKey = "films",       label = "Movies",       tickLabelStyle = list(         fontSize = "0.5em"       )     )   ),   series = list(     list(dataKey = "male", label = "Male"),     list(dataKey = "female", label = "Female"),     list(dataKey = "hermaphroditic", label = "Hermaphroditic"),     list(dataKey = "none", label = "None")   ),   grid = list(horizontal = TRUE),   height = 300 )

title: Bar Charts
text: Stack series top using stack property:
code: BarChart(   dataset = starwars_gender,   xAxis = list(     list(       scaleType = "band",       dataKey = "films",       label = "Movies",       tickLabelStyle = list(         fontSize = "0.5em"       )     )   ),   series = list(     list(dataKey = "male", label = "Male", stack = "total"),     list(dataKey = "female", label = "Female", stack = "total"),     list(dataKey = "hermaphroditic", label = "Hermaphroditic", stack = "total"),     list(dataKey = "none", label = "None", stack = "total")   ),   grid = list(horizontal = TRUE),   height = 300 )

title: Bar Charts
text: Customize colors, border radius, spacing:
code: BarChart(   dataset = starwars_gender,   xAxis = list(     list(       scaleType = "band",       dataKey = "films",       label = "Movies",       categoryGapRatio = 0.3,       barGapRatio = 0.5,       height = 100,       tickLabelStyle = list(         fontSize = "0.6em",         angle = 45       )     )   ),   series = list(     list(dataKey = "male", label = "Male", color = "#440154FF"),     list(dataKey = "female", label = "Female", color = "#2A788EFF"),     list(dataKey = "hermaphroditic", label = "Hermaphroditic", color = "#FDE725FF"),     list(dataKey = "none", label = "None", color = "#35B779FF")   ),   borderRadius = 10,   grid = list(horizontal = TRUE),   height = 400 )

title: Bar Charts
text: Display values directly bars:
code: starwars_top5 <- starwars_species |>   slice_head(n = 5)  BarChart(   dataset = starwars_top5,   xAxis = list(     list(       scaleType = "band",       dataKey = "species",       label = "Species"     )   ),   series = list(     list(dataKey = "n", label = "Count", barLabel = "value")   ),   height = 300 )

title: Bar Charts
text: Create different stacking groups:
code: BarChart(   dataset = starwars_gender,   xAxis = list(     list(       scaleType = "band",       dataKey = "films",       label = "Movies",       tickLabelStyle = list(         fontSize = "0.5em"       )     )   ),   series = list(     list(dataKey = "male", label = "Male", stack = "group1"),     list(dataKey = "female", label = "Female", stack = "group2"),     list(dataKey = "hermaphroditic", label = "Hermaphroditic", stack = "group3"),     list(dataKey = "none", label = "None", stack = "group3")   ),   grid = list(horizontal = TRUE),   height = 300 )

title: Bar Charts
text: Control spacing bars categories:
code: # Prepare height data starwars_height <- dplyr::starwars |>   filter(!is.na(height), !is.na(species)) |>   group_by(species) |>   summarize(avg_height = mean(height, na.rm = TRUE)) |>   arrange(desc(avg_height)) |>   slice_head(n = 6)  BarChart(   dataset = starwars_height,   xAxis = list(     list(       scaleType = "band",       dataKey = "species",       label = "Species",       categoryGapRatio = 0.2,  # Gap between categories       barGapRatio = 0.1        # Gap between bars     )   ),   series = list(     list(dataKey = "avg_height", label = "Average Height (cm)")   ),   height = 300 )

title: Bar Charts
text: BarElement BarLabel sub-components BarPlot uses internally render bar label. Call directly explore props: Use slotProps BarPlot pass props components without custom slot override:
code: BarElement(skipAnimation = TRUE) BarLabel(placement = "outside") ChartDataProvider(   dataset = starwars_top5,   height = 320,   xAxis = list(list(scaleType = "band", dataKey = "species", label = "Species")),   series = list(list(type = "bar", dataKey = "n", label = "Count")),   ChartsSurface(     BarPlot(       barLabel = "value",       slotProps = list(         barLabel = list(placement = "outside")       )     ),     ChartsXAxis(),     ChartsYAxis()   ) )

title: Bar Charts
text: MUI Bar Charts Documentation See also: Tooltip value formatters, Styling colors, Composition advanced layout, Axis & Grid grid axis options.

title: Composition
text: Self-contained components like BarChart simple use, composition API gives : Full control sub-components rendered Ability mix chart types (lines + bars one chart) Custom layout HTML components (legend, tooltip) Adding reference lines, custom text, overlays

title: Composition
text: composition pattern uses three key components: ChartDataProvider — wraps everything, provides data context ChartsSurface — renders SVG element (plots, axes, grid) ChartsLegend / ChartsTooltip — HTML components placed outside SVG
code: starwars_gender <- dplyr::starwars |>   unnest_longer(films) |>   count(films, sex) |>   filter(!is.na(sex)) |>   pivot_wider(names_from = sex, values_from = n) |>   mutate(release = c(1977, 2002, 1983, 2005, 1980, 2015, 1999)) |>   arrange(release)  ChartDataProvider(   dataset = starwars_gender,   height = 300,   xAxis = list(     list(       scaleType = "band",       dataKey = "films",       tickLabelStyle = list(fontSize = "0.5em")     )   ),   series = list(     list(type = "bar", dataKey = "male", label = "Male"),     list(type = "bar", dataKey = "female", label = "Female"),     list(type = "bar", dataKey = "hermaphroditic", label = "Hermaphroditic"),     list(type = "bar", dataKey = "none", label = "None")   ),   ChartsLegend(),   ChartsTooltip(),   ChartsSurface(     ChartsGrid(horizontal = TRUE),     BarPlot(),     ChartsXAxis(),     ChartsYAxis()   ) )

title: Composition
text: Use muiMaterial components custom titles layout:
code: Box(   sx = list(     width = "100%",     display = "flex",     flexDirection = "column",     alignItems = "center"   ),   Typography(variant = "h6", "Star Wars Characters by Gender"),   ChartDataProvider(     dataset = starwars_gender,     height = 300,     xAxis = list(       list(         scaleType = "band",         dataKey = "films",         tickLabelStyle = list(fontSize = "0.5em")       )     ),     series = list(       list(type = "bar", dataKey = "male", label = "Male"),       list(type = "bar", dataKey = "female", label = "Female")     ),     ChartsLegend(),     ChartsTooltip(),     ChartsSurface(       ChartsGrid(horizontal = TRUE),       BarPlot(),       ChartsXAxis(),       ChartsYAxis()     )   ) )

title: Composition
text: Combine different plot types single chart. Use type series specify plot kinds:
code: ChartDataProvider(   dataset = starwars_gender,   height = 350,   xAxis = list(     list(       scaleType = "band",       dataKey = "films",       tickLabelStyle = list(fontSize = "0.5em")     )   ),   series = list(     list(type = "bar", dataKey = "male", label = "Male (bars)"),     list(type = "line", dataKey = "female", label = "Female (line)")   ),   ChartsLegend(),   ChartsTooltip(),   ChartsSurface(     ChartsGrid(horizontal = TRUE),     BarPlot(),     LinePlot(),     MarkPlot(),     ChartsXAxis(),     ChartsYAxis()   ) )

title: Composition
text: Add reference lines mark important thresholds:
code: avg_male <- round(mean(starwars_gender$male, na.rm = TRUE))  ChartDataProvider(   dataset = starwars_gender,   height = 350,   xAxis = list(     list(       scaleType = "band",       dataKey = "films",       tickLabelStyle = list(fontSize = "0.5em")     )   ),   series = list(     list(type = "bar", dataKey = "male", label = "Male")   ),   ChartsTooltip(),   ChartsSurface(     ChartsGrid(horizontal = TRUE),     BarPlot(),     ChartsReferenceLine(       y = avg_male,       label = paste("Average:", avg_male),       lineStyle = list(stroke = "red", strokeDasharray = "5 5")     ),     ChartsXAxis(),     ChartsYAxis()   ) )

title: Composition
text: Compose scatter chart title custom grid:
code: starwars_scatter <- starwars |>   filter(!is.na(height), !is.na(mass), mass < 200) |>   select(name, height, mass) |>   mutate(id = row_number())  Box(   sx = list(     width = "100%",     display = "flex",     flexDirection = "column",     alignItems = "center"   ),   Typography(variant = "h6", "Star Wars Characters: Height vs Mass"),   ChartDataProvider(     dataset = starwars_scatter,     height = 350,     series = list(       list(         type = "scatter",         datasetKeys = list(x = "height", y = "mass"),         label = "Characters"       )     ),     xAxis = list(list(label = "Height (cm)")),     yAxis = list(list(label = "Mass (kg)")),     ChartsLegend(),     ChartsTooltip(trigger = "item"),     ChartsSurface(       ChartsGrid(horizontal = TRUE, vertical = TRUE),       ScatterPlot(),       ChartsXAxis(),       ChartsYAxis()     )   ) )

title: Composition
text: Compose line chart marks, area, highlighted points:
code: ChartDataProvider(   dataset = starwars_gender,   height = 350,   xAxis = list(     list(       scaleType = "point",       dataKey = "films",       tickLabelStyle = list(fontSize = "0.5em")     )   ),   series = list(     list(type = "line", dataKey = "male", label = "Male", area = TRUE),     list(type = "line", dataKey = "female", label = "Female", area = TRUE)   ),   ChartsLegend(),   ChartsTooltip(trigger = "axis"),   ChartsSurface(     ChartsGrid(horizontal = TRUE),     AreaPlot(),     LinePlot(),     MarkPlot(),     LineHighlightPlot(),     ChartsAxisHighlight(x = "line"),     ChartsXAxis(),     ChartsYAxis()   ) )

title: Composition
text: Compose gauge individual sub-components full control:
code: GaugeContainer(   width = 200,   height = 200,   value = 75,   startAngle = -110,   endAngle = 110,   innerRadius = "60%",   outerRadius = "100%",   cornerRadius = "50%",   GaugeReferenceArc(),   GaugeValueArc(),   GaugeValueText(     text = JS("({ value, valueMax }) => `${value} / ${valueMax}`")   ) )

title: Composition
text: ChartsContainer ChartsDataProvider v8 equivalents ChartContainer ChartDataProvider. ChartsDataProvider provides data context without rendering SVG — pair ChartsWrapper HTML layer ChartsSurface SVG: ChartsContainer combines ChartsDataProvider ChartsSurface single SVG-based container. Use need SVG content without outer HTML wrapper:
code: ChartsDataProvider(   dataset = starwars_gender,   height = 300,   xAxis = list(     list(       scaleType = "band",       dataKey = "films",       tickLabelStyle = list(fontSize = "0.5em")     )   ),   series = list(     list(type = "bar", dataKey = "male", label = "Male"),     list(type = "bar", dataKey = "female", label = "Female")   ),   ChartsWrapper(     ChartsLegend(),     ChartsTooltip(),     ChartsSurface(       ChartsGrid(horizontal = TRUE),       BarPlot(),       ChartsXAxis(),       ChartsYAxis()     )   ) ) ChartsContainer(   dataset = starwars_gender,   height = 300,   xAxis = list(     list(       scaleType = "band",       dataKey = "films",       tickLabelStyle = list(fontSize = "0.5em")     )   ),   series = list(     list(type = "bar", dataKey = "male", label = "Male"),     list(type = "bar", dataKey = "female", label = "Female")   ),   ChartsGrid(horizontal = TRUE),   BarPlot(),   ChartsAxisHighlight(x = "band"),   ChartsXAxis(),   ChartsYAxis() )

title: Composition
text: ChartsLabel renders styled HTML <span> element. Place sibling ChartsSurface (outside SVG) inside ChartDataProvider, pass text child argument:
code: ChartDataProvider(   dataset = starwars_gender,   height = 320,   xAxis = list(     list(       scaleType = "band",       dataKey = "films",       tickLabelStyle = list(fontSize = "0.5em")     )   ),   series = list(     list(type = "bar", dataKey = "male", label = "Male")   ),   ChartsLabel(     "Star Wars Characters",     style = list(fontSize = "0.85rem", fontWeight = "bold")   ),   ChartsSurface(     BarPlot(),     ChartsXAxis(),     ChartsYAxis()   ) )

title: Composition
text: ChartsText renders SVG text element inside ChartsSurface. Use add text annotations directly chart:
code: ChartDataProvider(   dataset = starwars_gender,   height = 320,   xAxis = list(     list(       scaleType = "band",       dataKey = "films",       tickLabelStyle = list(fontSize = "0.5em")     )   ),   series = list(     list(type = "bar", dataKey = "male", label = "Male")   ),   ChartsSurface(     BarPlot(),     ChartsText(       text = "Male characters per film",       x = 5,       y = 15,       style = list(fontSize = "0.75rem", fill = "#555")     ),     ChartsXAxis(),     ChartsYAxis()   ) )

title: Composition
text: ChartsClipPath adds SVG <clipPath> definition inside ChartsSurface restrict plot rendering drawing area:
code: ChartDataProvider(   dataset = starwars_gender,   height = 300,   xAxis = list(     list(       scaleType = "band",       dataKey = "films",       tickLabelStyle = list(fontSize = "0.5em")     )   ),   series = list(     list(type = "bar", dataKey = "male", label = "Male"),     list(type = "bar", dataKey = "female", label = "Female")   ),   ChartsSurface(     ChartsClipPath(id = "drawing-area-clip"),     BarPlot(),     ChartsXAxis(),     ChartsYAxis()   ) )

title: Composition
text: details composition, visit MUI X Composition documentation.

title: Gauge Charts
text: Gauge displays numeric value 0 100 default. can customize arc’s angle range.
code: # Calculate percentage of human characters human_pct <- starwars |>   summarise(pct = sum(species == "Human", na.rm = TRUE) / n() * 100) |>   pull(pct) |>   round(0)  # Full circle gauge Gauge(   width = 100,   height = 100,   value = human_pct ) # Semi-circle gauge Gauge(   width = 100,   height = 100,   value = human_pct,   startAngle = -90,   endAngle = 90 )

title: Gauge Charts
text: Gauge’s value 0 100 default. Use valueMin valueMax modify .
code: # Average character height (170-180 range) avg_height <- starwars |>   filter(!is.na(height)) |>   summarise(avg = mean(height)) |>   pull(avg) |>   round(0)  # Default range (0-100) Gauge(   width = 100,   height = 100,   value = 50 ) # Custom range (150-200 cm) Gauge(   width = 100,   height = 100,   value = avg_height,   valueMin = 150,   valueMax = 200 )

title: Gauge Charts
text: Modify arc shape startAngle, endAngle, innerRadius, outerRadius, cornerRadius.
code: # Calculate character completion rate completion_rate <- starwars |>   summarise(rate = sum(!is.na(height) & !is.na(mass) & !is.na(birth_year)) / n() * 100) |>   pull(rate) |>   round(0)  # Donut gauge Gauge(   width = 150,   height = 150,   value = completion_rate,   innerRadius = "70%",   outerRadius = "100%" ) # With corner radius Gauge(   width = 150,   height = 150,   value = completion_rate,   innerRadius = "70%",   outerRadius = "100%",   cornerRadius = "50%" )

title: Gauge Charts
text: default, Gauge displays value center. Use text prop customize .
code: # Percentage of characters with known species known_species_pct <- starwars |>   summarise(pct = sum(!is.na(species)) / n() * 100) |>   pull(pct) |>   round(0)  # Custom text Gauge(   width = 150,   height = 150,   value = known_species_pct,   text = paste0(known_species_pct, "%") ) # Using a formatter with JS Gauge(   width = 150,   height = 150,   value = known_species_pct,   text = JS("({ value, valueMax }) => `${value} / ${valueMax}`") )

title: Gauge Charts
text: Customize Gauge styles using sx prop MuiGauge CSS class selectors:
code: Gauge(   width = 150,   height = 150,   value = 75,   cornerRadius = "50%",   sx = list(     "& .MuiGauge-valueText" = list(       fontSize = 40     ),     "& .MuiGauge-valueArc" = list(       fill = "#52b202"     ),     "& .MuiGauge-referenceArc" = list(       fill = "#e0e0e0"     )   ) )

title: Gauge Charts
text: Create semi-circle quarter-circle gauges different use cases.
code: # Character power level power_level <- 85  # Semi-circle (speedometer style) Gauge(   width = 200,   height = 120,   value = power_level,   startAngle = -90,   endAngle = 90 ) # Quarter-circle Gauge(   width = 150,   height = 150,   value = power_level,   startAngle = 0,   endAngle = 90 )

title: Gauge Charts
text: Create dashboard multiple gauges showing different metrics.
code: library(muiMaterial) #>  #> Attaching package: 'muiMaterial' #> The following object is masked from 'package:muiCharts': #>  #>     Toolbar  # Calculate various metrics human_pct <- round(sum(starwars$species == "Human", na.rm = TRUE) / nrow(starwars) * 100, 0) known_height_pct <- round(sum(!is.na(starwars$height)) / nrow(starwars) * 100, 0) known_mass_pct <- round(sum(!is.na(starwars$mass)) / nrow(starwars) * 100, 0)  Box(   sx = list(     display = "flex",     gap = 2,     justifyContent = "center",     alignItems = "center"   ),   Box(     sx = list(       display = "flex",       flexDirection = "column",       alignItems = "center"     ),     Typography(variant = "subtitle2", "Human Characters"),     Gauge(       width = 100,       height = 100,       value = human_pct     )   ),   Box(     sx = list(       display = "flex",       flexDirection = "column",       alignItems = "center"     ),     Typography(variant = "subtitle2", "Known Height"),     Gauge(       width = 100,       height = 100,       value = known_height_pct     )   ),   Box(     sx = list(       display = "flex",       flexDirection = "column",       alignItems = "center"     ),     Typography(variant = "subtitle2", "Known Mass"),     Gauge(       width = 100,       height = 100,       value = known_mass_pct     )   ) )

title: Gauge Charts
text: Gauge compliant ARIA Meter pattern. can add aria-label aria-labelledby better accessibility.
code: # Character diversity index diversity_index <- starwars |>   count(species) |>   summarise(index = n()) |>   pull(index)  # Scale to 0-100 diversity_pct <- min(diversity_index * 2, 100)  Box(   Typography(     id = "diversity_label",     variant = "h6",     "Species Diversity Index"   ),   Gauge(     width = 150,     height = 150,     value = diversity_pct,     `aria-labelledby` = "diversity_label",     `aria-valuetext` = paste0(diversity_index, " unique species")   ) )

title: Gauge Charts
text: advanced customization, use GaugeContainer individual sub-components: can customize text display using GaugeValueText JS formatter:
code: # Custom gauge using composition GaugeContainer(   width = 200,   height = 200,   value = human_pct,   startAngle = -110,   endAngle = 110,   GaugeReferenceArc(),   GaugeValueArc(),   GaugeValueText() ) GaugeContainer(   width = 200,   height = 200,   value = 75,   startAngle = -110,   endAngle = 110,   innerRadius = "60%",   outerRadius = "100%",   cornerRadius = "50%",   GaugeReferenceArc(),   GaugeValueArc(),   GaugeValueText(     text = JS("({ value, valueMax }) => `${value} / ${valueMax}`")   ) )

title: Gauge Charts
text: MUI Gauge Chart Documentation See also: Styling sx overrides arc styling.

title: Highlighting
text: highlightScope prop controls items emphasized hover. two properties: highlight — items highlight: "item", "series", "none" fade — items fade: "global", "series", "none"

title: Highlighting
text: Highlight single item hover, fading others:
code: starwars_gender <- dplyr::starwars |>   unnest_longer(films) |>   count(films, sex) |>   filter(!is.na(sex)) |>   pivot_wider(names_from = sex, values_from = n) |>   mutate(release = c(1977, 2002, 1983, 2005, 1980, 2015, 1999)) |>   arrange(release)  BarChart(   dataset = starwars_gender,   xAxis = list(     list(scaleType = "band", dataKey = "films", tickLabelStyle = list(fontSize = "0.5em"))   ),   series = list(     list(       dataKey = "male",       label = "Male",       highlightScope = list(highlight = "item", fade = "global")     ),     list(       dataKey = "female",       label = "Female",       highlightScope = list(highlight = "item", fade = "global")     )   ),   height = 300 )

title: Highlighting
text: Highlight entire series hovering item :
code: BarChart(   dataset = starwars_gender,   xAxis = list(     list(scaleType = "band", dataKey = "films", tickLabelStyle = list(fontSize = "0.5em"))   ),   series = list(     list(       dataKey = "male",       label = "Male",       highlightScope = list(highlight = "series", fade = "global")     ),     list(       dataKey = "female",       label = "Female",       highlightScope = list(highlight = "series", fade = "global")     ),     list(       dataKey = "hermaphroditic",       label = "Hermaphroditic",       highlightScope = list(highlight = "series", fade = "global")     )   ),   height = 300 )

title: Highlighting
text: Customize appearance faded items using faded property pie charts:
code: df <- starwars |>   count(sex) |>   filter(!is.na(sex)) |>   select(label = sex, value = n)  PieChart(   series = list(     list(       data = df,       highlightScope = list(highlight = "item", fade = "global"),       faded = list(         innerRadius = 30,         additionalRadius = -30,         color = "gray"       )     )   ),   height = 300 )

title: Highlighting
text: Highlighting works line charts :
code: LineChart(   dataset = starwars_gender,   xAxis = list(     list(scaleType = "point", dataKey = "films", tickLabelStyle = list(fontSize = "0.5em"))   ),   series = list(     list(       dataKey = "male",       label = "Male",       highlightScope = list(highlight = "series", fade = "global")     ),     list(       dataKey = "female",       label = "Female",       highlightScope = list(highlight = "series", fade = "global")     )   ),   height = 300 )

title: Highlighting
text: Scatter charts also support highlighting series:
code: df_male <- starwars |>   filter(sex == "male", !is.na(mass), !is.na(height)) |>   select(x = mass, y = height)  df_female <- starwars |>   filter(sex == "female", !is.na(mass), !is.na(height)) |>   select(x = mass, y = height)  ScatterChart(   series = list(     list(       data = df_male,       label = "Male",       highlightScope = list(highlight = "series", fade = "global")     ),     list(       data = df_female,       label = "Female",       highlightScope = list(highlight = "series", fade = "global")     )   ),   xAxis = list(list(min = 0, label = "Mass (kg)")),   yAxis = list(list(min = 0, label = "Height (cm)")),   height = 350 )

title: Highlighting
text: using composition, add ChartsAxisHighlight display highlight band line hover:
code: ChartDataProvider(   dataset = starwars_gender,   height = 300,   xAxis = list(     list(scaleType = "band", dataKey = "films", tickLabelStyle = list(fontSize = "0.5em"))   ),   series = list(     list(type = "bar", dataKey = "male", label = "Male"),     list(type = "bar", dataKey = "female", label = "Female")   ),   ChartsTooltip(trigger = "axis"),   ChartsSurface(     BarPlot(),     ChartsAxisHighlight(x = "band"),     ChartsXAxis(),     ChartsYAxis()   ) )

title: Highlighting
text: ChartsAxisHighlightPath SVG path element rendered ChartsAxisHighlight. Pass via slots apply custom styles:
code: ChartDataProvider(   dataset = starwars_gender,   height = 300,   xAxis = list(     list(scaleType = "band", dataKey = "films", tickLabelStyle = list(fontSize = "0.5em"))   ),   series = list(     list(type = "bar", dataKey = "male", label = "Male"),     list(type = "bar", dataKey = "female", label = "Female")   ),   ChartsTooltip(trigger = "axis"),   ChartsSurface(     BarPlot(),     ChartsAxisHighlight(       x = "band",       slots = list(axisHighlight = JS("         React.forwardRef(function StyledHighlight(props, ref) {           return React.createElement(             MuiXCharts.ChartsAxisHighlightPath,             Object.assign({}, props, {               ref: ref,               style: { fill: 'rgba(255, 200, 0, 0.2)', stroke: 'orange', strokeWidth: 1 }             })           );         })       "))     ),     ChartsXAxis(),     ChartsYAxis()   ) )

title: Highlighting
text: FocusedBar, FocusedLineMark, FocusedScatterMark render visual indicator chart element receives keyboard focus. Add inside ChartsSurface building charts composition API: line charts, use FocusedLineMark: scatter charts, use FocusedScatterMark:
code: ChartDataProvider(   dataset = starwars_gender,   height = 300,   xAxis = list(     list(scaleType = "band", dataKey = "films", tickLabelStyle = list(fontSize = "0.5em"))   ),   series = list(     list(type = "bar", dataKey = "male", label = "Male"),     list(type = "bar", dataKey = "female", label = "Female")   ),   ChartsTooltip(),   ChartsSurface(     BarPlot(),     FocusedBar(),     ChartsXAxis(),     ChartsYAxis()   ) ) ChartDataProvider(   dataset = starwars_gender,   height = 300,   xAxis = list(     list(scaleType = "point", dataKey = "films", tickLabelStyle = list(fontSize = "0.5em"))   ),   series = list(     list(type = "line", dataKey = "male", label = "Male"),     list(type = "line", dataKey = "female", label = "Female")   ),   ChartsTooltip(),   ChartsSurface(     LinePlot(),     MarkPlot(),     FocusedLineMark(),     ChartsXAxis(),     ChartsYAxis()   ) ) starwars_scatter <- starwars |>   filter(!is.na(height), !is.na(mass), mass < 200) |>   select(height, mass) |>   mutate(id = row_number())  ChartDataProvider(   dataset = starwars_scatter,   height = 300,   series = list(     list(type = "scatter", datasetKeys = list(x = "height", y = "mass"), label = "Characters")   ),   xAxis = list(list(label = "Height (cm)")),   yAxis = list(list(label = "Mass (kg)")),   ChartsTooltip(trigger = "item"),   ChartsSurface(     ScatterPlot(),     FocusedScatterMark(),     ChartsXAxis(),     ChartsYAxis()   ) )

title: Highlighting
text: LineHighlightElement component rendered LineHighlightPlot hover indicator line chart. Call inspect props: Use slotProps LineHighlightPlot configure highlight circle size:
code: LineHighlightElement() ChartDataProvider(   dataset = starwars_gender,   height = 300,   xAxis = list(     list(scaleType = "point", dataKey = "films", tickLabelStyle = list(fontSize = "0.5em"))   ),   series = list(     list(type = "line", dataKey = "male", label = "Male"),     list(type = "line", dataKey = "female", label = "Female")   ),   ChartsTooltip(trigger = "axis"),   ChartsSurface(     LinePlot(),     MarkPlot(),     LineHighlightPlot(slotProps = list(highlight = list(r = 7))),     ChartsXAxis(),     ChartsYAxis()   ) )

title: Highlighting
text: details highlighting, visit MUI X Highlighting documentation.

title: Introduction
text: can install development version muiCharts like :
code: remotes::install_github("lgnbhl/muiCharts")

title: Introduction
code: library(muiCharts) library(dplyr)  starwars_summary <- dplyr::starwars |>   filter(species %in% c("Human", "Droid", "Wookiee")) |>   group_by(species) |>   summarise(height = mean(height, na.rm = TRUE), mass = mean(mass, na.rm = TRUE)) BarChart(   dataset = starwars_summary,   xAxis = list(     list(       scaleType = "band",       dataKey = "species",       label = "Species"     )   ),   series = list(     list(dataKey = "height", label = "Height (cm)"),     list(dataKey = "mass", label = "Mass (kg)")   ),   grid = list(horizontal = TRUE),   height = 300 )

title: Introduction
text: Use JS() function pass JavaScript code, example customize tooltip values valueFormatter:
code: customValueFormatter <- function(category){   JS(     paste0(       "function(value){",       "if (value === null) { return 'None' }",       "return `${value} ", category, "`}"     )   ) }  BarChart(   dataset = starwars_summary,   xAxis = list(     list(       scaleType = "band",       dataKey = "species",       label = "Species",       valueFormatter = JS("(value) => `${value}`")     )   ),   series = list(     list(       dataKey = "height",       label = "Height (cm)",       valueFormatter = customValueFormatter("cm")     ),     list(       dataKey = "mass",       label = "Mass (kg)",       valueFormatter = customValueFormatter("kg")     )   ),   grid = list(horizontal = TRUE),   height = 400 )

title: Introduction
text: muiCharts provides following chart types, dedicated article: Bar Charts — vertical horizontal bar charts grouping stacking Line Charts — line charts marks, curves, null handling Area Charts — filled area charts stacking baselines Pie Charts — pie donut charts arc labels Scatter Charts — scatter plots Voronoi interaction Radar Charts — radar charts multi-metric comparisons Sparkline Charts — compact inline charts Gauge Charts — gauge displays single metrics

title: Introduction
text: Cross-cutting features apply chart types: Axis & Grid — scale types, labels, value formatters, grid lines, margins Tooltip — tooltip triggers, formatting, composition Legend — legend position, direction, color legends Highlighting — highlight fade interactions Styling — colors, color maps, CSS overrides sx Composition — build charts individual sub-components

title: Introduction
text: Shiny — reactive charts, click events, dashboards Quarto — HTML documents, dashboards, tabsets

title: Introduction
text: issue, question want contribute pull request, don’t hesitate write https://github.com/lgnbhl/muiCharts updates follow Felix Luginbuhl LinkedIn.

title: Legend
text: default, legend displayed chart showing series:
code: starwars_gender <- dplyr::starwars |>   unnest_longer(films) |>   count(films, sex) |>   filter(!is.na(sex)) |>   pivot_wider(names_from = sex, values_from = n) |>   mutate(release = c(1977, 2002, 1983, 2005, 1980, 2015, 1999)) |>   arrange(release)  BarChart(   dataset = starwars_gender,   xAxis = list(     list(scaleType = "band", dataKey = "films", tickLabelStyle = list(fontSize = "0.5em"))   ),   series = list(     list(dataKey = "male", label = "Male"),     list(dataKey = "female", label = "Female"),     list(dataKey = "hermaphroditic", label = "Hermaphroditic"),     list(dataKey = "none", label = "None")   ),   height = 300 )

title: Legend
text: Use hideLegend = TRUE remove legend:
code: BarChart(   dataset = starwars_gender,   xAxis = list(     list(scaleType = "band", dataKey = "films", tickLabelStyle = list(fontSize = "0.5em"))   ),   series = list(     list(dataKey = "male", label = "Male"),     list(dataKey = "female", label = "Female")   ),   hideLegend = TRUE,   height = 300 )

title: Legend
text: Customize legend position using slotProps. legend can positioned "start", "middle", "end" horizontal vertical directions:
code: BarChart(   dataset = starwars_gender,   xAxis = list(     list(scaleType = "band", dataKey = "films", tickLabelStyle = list(fontSize = "0.5em"))   ),   series = list(     list(dataKey = "male", label = "Male"),     list(dataKey = "female", label = "Female")   ),   slotProps = list(     legend = list(       position = list(         vertical = "middle",         horizontal = "right"       )     )   ),   height = 300 )

title: Legend
text: Change legend layout direction horizontal (default) vertical:
code: BarChart(   dataset = starwars_gender,   xAxis = list(     list(scaleType = "band", dataKey = "films", tickLabelStyle = list(fontSize = "0.5em"))   ),   series = list(     list(dataKey = "male", label = "Male"),     list(dataKey = "female", label = "Female"),     list(dataKey = "hermaphroditic", label = "Hermaphroditic"),     list(dataKey = "none", label = "None")   ),   slotProps = list(     legend = list(       direction = "vertical"     )   ),   height = 350 )

title: Legend
text: Pie charts automatically show legend entries slice:
code: df <- starwars |>   count(sex) |>   filter(!is.na(sex)) |>   select(label = sex, value = n)  PieChart(   series = list(     list(data = df)   ),   height = 300 )

title: Legend
text: charts using colorMap, can use ContinuousColorLegend PiecewiseColorLegend:

title: Legend
code: starwars_color <- starwars |>   filter(!is.na(height), !is.na(mass), !is.na(birth_year), mass < 200) |>   select(name, height, mass, birth_year) |>   mutate(id = row_number())  ScatterChart(   height = 350,   dataset = starwars_color,   series = list(     list(       datasetKeys = list(x = "height", y = "mass", z = "birth_year"),       label = "Characters"     )   ),   xAxis = list(list(label = "Height (cm)")),   yAxis = list(list(label = "Mass (kg)")),   zAxis = list(     list(       colorMap = list(         type = "continuous",         min = 0,         max = 100,         color = c("#d01c8b", "#4dac26")       )     )   ) )

title: Legend
text: using composition, place ChartsLegend inside ChartDataProvider outside ChartsSurface (since MUI v8 legend HTML component, SVG):
code: library(muiMaterial) #>  #> Attaching package: 'muiMaterial' #> The following object is masked from 'package:muiCharts': #>  #>     Toolbar  ChartDataProvider(   dataset = starwars_gender,   height = 300,   xAxis = list(     list(scaleType = "band", dataKey = "films", tickLabelStyle = list(fontSize = "0.5em"))   ),   series = list(     list(type = "bar", dataKey = "male", label = "Male"),     list(type = "bar", dataKey = "female", label = "Female")   ),   ChartsLegend(),   ChartsSurface(     BarPlot(),     ChartsXAxis(),     ChartsYAxis()   ) )

title: Legend
text: ChartsLabelMark renders colored shape used identify series — small icon appears next series name. Use inside custom legend layouts annotations:
code: library(muiMaterial)  ChartDataProvider(   dataset = starwars_gender,   height = 300,   xAxis = list(     list(scaleType = "band", dataKey = "films", tickLabelStyle = list(fontSize = "0.5em"))   ),   series = list(     list(type = "bar", dataKey = "male", label = "Male"),     list(type = "bar", dataKey = "female", label = "Female")   ),   Box(     sx = list(display = "flex", gap = "16px", padding = "8px"),     Box(       sx = list(display = "flex", alignItems = "center", gap = "4px"),       ChartsLabelMark(type = "square", color = "#19d23e"),       Typography(variant = "body2", "Male")     ),     Box(       sx = list(display = "flex", alignItems = "center", gap = "4px"),       ChartsLabelMark(type = "square", color = "#9c27b0"),       Typography(variant = "body2", "Female")     )   ),   ChartsSurface(     BarPlot(),     ChartsXAxis(),     ChartsYAxis()   ) )

title: Legend
text: details legend customization, visit MUI X Legend documentation.

title: Line Charts
text: Line charts ideal showing values change continuous dimensions time measurement scales. emphasize trends, patterns, fluctuations, making useful exploring relationships, detecting cycles, tracking performance time.

title: Line Charts
code: library(muiCharts) library(dplyr) library(tidyr)

title: Line Charts
text: ’ll use starwars dataset create various line chart examples:
code: # Count characters by gender across films starwars_gender <- dplyr::starwars |>   unnest_longer(films) |>   count(films, sex) |>   filter(!is.na(sex)) |>   pivot_wider(names_from = sex, values_from = n, values_fill = 0) |>   mutate(release = c(1977, 2002, 1983, 2005, 1980, 2015, 1999)) |>   arrange(release)  # Character counts by birth year (aggregated into bins) starwars_birth <- dplyr::starwars |>   filter(!is.na(birth_year)) |>   mutate(birth_decade = floor(birth_year / 10) * 10) |>   count(birth_decade) |>   arrange(birth_decade)

title: Line Charts
text: simple line chart showing character counts gender across movies:
code: LineChart(   dataset = starwars_gender,   xAxis = list(     list(       scaleType = "point",       dataKey = "films",       label = "Movies",       tickLabelStyle = list(         fontSize = "0.5em"       )     )   ),   series = list(     list(dataKey = "male", label = "Male"),     list(dataKey = "female", label = "Female"),     list(dataKey = "hermaphroditic", label = "Hermaphroditic"),     list(dataKey = "none", label = "None")   ),   height = 300 )

title: Line Charts
text: Use numeric values x-axis linear scale:
code: LineChart(   dataset = starwars_gender,   xAxis = list(     list(       scaleType = "linear",       dataKey = "release",       label = "Release Year",       valueFormatter = JS("(value) => String(value)")     )   ),   series = list(     list(dataKey = "male", label = "Male"),     list(dataKey = "female", label = "Female")   ),   grid = list(horizontal = TRUE),   height = 300 )

title: Line Charts
text: Display multiple lines compare different series:
code: LineChart(   dataset = starwars_gender,   xAxis = list(     list(       scaleType = "point",       dataKey = "films",       label = "Movies",       tickLabelStyle = list(         fontSize = "0.5em"       )     )   ),   series = list(     list(dataKey = "male", label = "Male", showMark = TRUE),     list(dataKey = "female", label = "Female", showMark = TRUE),     list(dataKey = "hermaphroditic", label = "Hermaphroditic", showMark = TRUE),     list(dataKey = "none", label = "None", showMark = TRUE)   ),   height = 300 )

title: Line Charts
text: Show data points line using showMark property:
code: LineChart(   dataset = starwars_birth,   xAxis = list(     list(       scaleType = "linear",       dataKey = "birth_decade",       label = "Birth Decade (BBY)"     )   ),   series = list(     list(dataKey = "n", label = "Characters Born", showMark = TRUE)   ),   height = 300 )

title: Line Charts
text: Hide marks cleaner look:
code: LineChart(   dataset = starwars_gender,   xAxis = list(     list(       scaleType = "point",       dataKey = "films",       label = "Movies",       tickLabelStyle = list(         fontSize = "0.5em"       )     )   ),   series = list(     list(dataKey = "male", label = "Male", showMark = FALSE),     list(dataKey = "female", label = "Female", showMark = FALSE)   ),   grid = list(horizontal = TRUE, vertical = TRUE),   height = 300 )

title: Line Charts
text: Customize line interpolation using curve property:
code: LineChart(   dataset = starwars_gender,   xAxis = list(     list(       scaleType = "point",       dataKey = "films",       label = "Movies",       tickLabelStyle = list(         fontSize = "0.5em"       )     )   ),   series = list(     list(dataKey = "male", label = "Male", curve = "natural", showMark = TRUE),     list(dataKey = "female", label = "Female", curve = "linear", showMark = TRUE)   ),   height = 300 )

title: Line Charts
text: Use step interpolation categorical transitions:
code: LineChart(   dataset = starwars_gender,   xAxis = list(     list(       scaleType = "point",       dataKey = "films",       label = "Movies",       tickLabelStyle = list(         fontSize = "0.5em"       )     )   ),   series = list(     list(dataKey = "male", label = "Male", curve = "step")   ),   height = 300 )

title: Line Charts
text: Handle missing data null values:
code: # Create data with missing values starwars_incomplete <- starwars_gender |>   mutate(     male_partial = ifelse(row_number() <= 3, male, NA),     female_partial = ifelse(row_number() >= 3, female, NA)   )  LineChart(   dataset = starwars_incomplete,   xAxis = list(     list(       scaleType = "point",       dataKey = "films",       label = "Movies",       tickLabelStyle = list(         fontSize = "0.5em"       )     )   ),   series = list(     list(dataKey = "male_partial", label = "Male (Partial)", showMark = TRUE),     list(dataKey = "female_partial", label = "Female (Partial)", showMark = TRUE)   ),   height = 300 )

title: Line Charts
text: Connect lines across null values:
code: LineChart(   dataset = starwars_incomplete,   xAxis = list(     list(       scaleType = "point",       dataKey = "films",       label = "Movies",       tickLabelStyle = list(         fontSize = "0.5em"       )     )   ),   series = list(     list(dataKey = "male_partial", label = "Male (Connected)", showMark = TRUE, connectNulls = TRUE),     list(dataKey = "female_partial", label = "Female (Connected)", showMark = TRUE, connectNulls = TRUE)   ),   height = 300 )

title: Line Charts
text: LineElement, MarkElement, AnimatedLine sub-components rendered LinePlot MarkPlot line, mark, animated line respectively. Call explore props: Use slotProps pass props components composition chart:
code: LineElement(skipAnimation = TRUE) MarkElement(shape = "circle") AnimatedLine(skipAnimation = TRUE) ChartDataProvider(   dataset = starwars_gender,   height = 300,   xAxis = list(     list(       scaleType = "point",       dataKey = "films",       tickLabelStyle = list(fontSize = "0.5em")     )   ),   series = list(     list(type = "line", dataKey = "male", label = "Male"),     list(type = "line", dataKey = "female", label = "Female")   ),   ChartsTooltip(),   ChartsSurface(     LinePlot(slotProps = list(line = list(skipAnimation = TRUE))),     MarkPlot(slotProps = list(mark = list(shape = "circle"))),     ChartsXAxis(),     ChartsYAxis()   ) )

title: Pie Charts
text: Pie charts express portions whole using arcs angles within circle. ideal showing proportions making relative shares easy compare glance. slice represents category’s contribution total.

title: Pie Charts
code: library(muiCharts) library(dplyr)

title: Pie Charts
text: pie charts, data must label value columns:
code: # Count characters by sex starwars_sex <- dplyr::starwars |>   count(sex) |>   filter(!is.na(sex)) |>   select(label = sex, value = n)  # Count characters by species (top 5) starwars_species <- dplyr::starwars |>   count(species) |>   filter(!is.na(species)) |>   arrange(desc(n)) |>   slice_head(n = 5) |>   select(label = species, value = n)

title: Pie Charts
text: simple pie chart showing distribution characters sex:
code: PieChart(   series = list(     list(       data = starwars_sex     )   ),   height = 300 )

title: Pie Charts
text: Add color column data assign colors individual slices:
code: starwars_sex_colored <- starwars_sex |>   mutate(color = c("#440154FF", "#2A788EFF", "#FDE725FF", "#35B779FF"))  PieChart(   series = list(     list(       data = starwars_sex_colored     )   ),   height = 300 )

title: Pie Charts
text: Create donut chart setting innerRadius:
code: PieChart(   series = list(     list(       data = starwars_sex,       innerRadius = 50,       outerRadius = 100     )   ),   height = 300 )

title: Pie Charts
text: Display values directly slices:
code: PieChart(   series = list(     list(       data = starwars_sex,       arcLabel = "label",       innerRadius = 50,       outerRadius = 100     )   ),   height = 300 )

title: Pie Charts
text: Show value arc:
code: PieChart(   series = list(     list(       data = starwars_species,       arcLabel = "value",       innerRadius = 40,       outerRadius = 100     )   ),   height = 300 )

title: Pie Charts
text: Display custom formatted labels using JavaScript:
code: # Calculate percentages starwars_sex_pct <- starwars_sex |>   mutate(     pct = round(value / sum(value) * 100, 1),     pct_label = paste0(pct, "%")   )  PieChart(   series = list(     list(       data = starwars_sex_pct,       arcLabel = JS("(item) => `${item.label}: ${item.pct}%`"),       arcLabelMinAngle = 20,       innerRadius = 50,       outerRadius = 100     )   ),   height = 300 )

title: Pie Charts
text: Add spacing slices using paddingAngle:
code: PieChart(   series = list(     list(       data = starwars_sex,       paddingAngle = 5,       cornerRadius = 5,       innerRadius = 40,       outerRadius = 100     )   ),   height = 300 )

title: Pie Charts
text: Create semi-circle chart setting start end angles:
code: PieChart(   series = list(     list(       data = starwars_sex,       startAngle = -90,       endAngle = 90,       innerRadius = 50,       outerRadius = 100     )   ),   height = 250 )

title: Pie Charts
text: Create nested pie charts multiple series:
code: # Prepare inner and outer data starwars_inner <- dplyr::starwars |>   count(sex) |>   filter(!is.na(sex)) |>   select(label = sex, value = n)  starwars_outer <- dplyr::starwars |>   filter(!is.na(sex), !is.na(species)) |>   count(species) |>   arrange(desc(n)) |>   slice_head(n = 8) |>   select(label = species, value = n)  PieChart(   series = list(     list(       data = starwars_inner,       innerRadius = 0,       outerRadius = 60     ),     list(       data = starwars_outer,       innerRadius = 80,       outerRadius = 100     )   ),   height = 300 )

title: Pie Charts
text: Control position size pie:
code: PieChart(   series = list(     list(       data = starwars_sex,       cx = 150,       cy = 150,       innerRadius = 40,       outerRadius = 80     )   ),   height = 300,   width = 300 )

title: Pie Charts
text: Create gauge-like visualization:
code: # Calculate percentage for a specific category starwars_gauge <- dplyr::starwars |>   count(sex) |>   filter(!is.na(sex)) |>   mutate(     prop = n / sum(n) * 100,     prop_label = paste0(round(prop), "%")   ) |>   filter(sex == "female") |>   select(label = sex, value = prop)  # Add remaining to complete the circle starwars_gauge_data <- bind_rows(   starwars_gauge,   list(label = "Other", value = 100 - starwars_gauge$value) )  PieChart(   series = list(     list(       data = starwars_gauge_data,       innerRadius = 70,       outerRadius = 90,       startAngle = -90,       endAngle = 90     )   ),   height = 250 )

title: Pie Charts
text: Build pie charts individual sub-components using ChartDataProvider. Use PiePlot render arcs labels together: Use PieArcPlot PieArcLabelPlot separately independent control arcs labels: PieArc PieArcLabel slot sub-components individual arc label rendering:
code: ChartDataProvider(   series = list(     list(       type = "pie",       data = starwars_sex,       innerRadius = 50,       outerRadius = 100     )   ),   height = 300,   ChartsTooltip(trigger = "item"),   ChartsSurface(     PiePlot()   ) ) ChartDataProvider(   series = list(     list(       type = "pie",       data = starwars_sex,       innerRadius = 50,       outerRadius = 100,       arcLabel = "label"     )   ),   height = 300,   ChartsTooltip(trigger = "item"),   ChartsSurface(     PieArcPlot(),     PieArcLabelPlot()   ) ) PieArc(skipAnimation = TRUE) PieArcLabel()

title: Quarto
text: muiCharts uses MUI (Material UI) rendering, can conflict Bootstrap CSS bundled Quarto. may cause visual issues broken layouts, unexpected margins, wrong font sizing.

title: Quarto
text: simplest fix disable Bootstrap entirely setting minimal: true YAML header: removes Bootstrap Quarto styling features (navbar, footer, search, etc.), giving MUI full control chart rendering. recommended approach standalone chart documents.
code: --- title: "My Charts" format:   html:     minimal: true ---

title: Quarto
text: want keep Quarto features disable Bootstrap, use theme: none: Note theme: none still strips Quarto structural elements depend Bootstrap (navigation, footer, logo). similar minimal: true sets theme: none automatically.
code: --- title: "My Charts" format:   html:     theme: none ---

title: Quarto
text: need keep Bootstrap (e.g., Quarto websites dashboards), can add targeted CSS fix specific conflicts: custom.css, scope overrides MUI chart containers: exact overrides needed depend Bootstrap styles conflict charts. Inspect rendered output identify specific issues.
code: --- title: "My Charts" format:   html:     css: custom.css --- .mui-charts-widget * {   box-sizing: content-box; } .mui-charts-widget label {   margin-bottom: 0; }

title: Quarto
text: dashboard format uses Bootstrap layout grid. experience conflicts, custom CSS approach (Solution 3) best option since minimal: true theme: none compatible dashboard format.

title: Quarto
text: muiCharts renders interactive HTML widgets, works Quarto format supports HTML output (html, dashboard, revealjs). static formats like PDF Word, charts render. Use format: html format: dashboard. Set height charts control sizing within Quarto layout containers.

title: Radar Charts
text: Create basic radar chart showing character counts sex across different metrics:
code: # Prepare data: count characters by sex for each film starwars_film_sex <- starwars |>   unnest_longer(films) |>   count(films, sex) |>   filter(!is.na(sex)) |>   pivot_wider(names_from = sex, values_from = n, values_fill = 0)  # Create radar chart for one film film_data <- starwars_film_sex |>   filter(films == "A New Hope")  RadarChart(   height = 300,   series = list(     list(       label = "A New Hope",       data = c(film_data$male, film_data$female, film_data$hermaphroditic, film_data$none)     )   ),   radar = list(     metrics = c("male", "female", "hermaphroditic", "none")   ) )

title: Radar Charts
text: Compare multiple films radar chart:
code: # Prepare data for three films film1 <- starwars_film_sex |> filter(films == "A New Hope") film2 <- starwars_film_sex |> filter(films == "Attack of the Clones") film3 <- starwars_film_sex |> filter(films == "Return of the Jedi")  RadarChart(   height = 350,   series = list(     list(       label = "A New Hope",       data = c(film1$male, film1$female, film1$hermaphroditic, film1$none)     ),     list(       label = "Attack of the Clones",       data = c(film2$male, film2$female, film2$hermaphroditic, film2$none)     ),     list(       label = "Return of the Jedi",       data = c(film3$male, film3$female, film3$hermaphroditic, film3$none)     )   ),   radar = list(     metrics = c("male", "female", "hermaphroditic", "none")   ) )

title: Radar Charts
text: Customize series appearance hideMark fillArea:
code: RadarChart(   height = 350,   series = list(     list(       label = "A New Hope",       data = c(film1$male, film1$female, film1$hermaphroditic, film1$none),       fillArea = TRUE     ),     list(       label = "Attack of the Clones",       data = c(film2$male, film2$female, film2$hermaphroditic, film2$none),       hideMark = TRUE     )   ),   radar = list(     metrics = c("male", "female", "hermaphroditic", "none")   ) )

title: Radar Charts
text: Define metrics detailed configuration using objects name, min, max:
code: RadarChart(   height = 350,   series = list(     list(       label = "A New Hope",       data = c(film1$male, film1$female, film1$hermaphroditic, film1$none)     ),     list(       label = "Attack of the Clones",       data = c(film2$male, film2$female, film2$hermaphroditic, film2$none)     ),     list(       label = "Return of the Jedi",       data = c(film3$male, film3$female, film3$hermaphroditic, film3$none)     )   ),   radar = list(     metrics = list(       list(name = "male", min = 0, max = 30),       list(name = "female", min = 0, max = 30),       list(name = "hermaphroditic", min = 0, max = 30),       list(name = "none", min = 0, max = 30)     )   ) )

title: Radar Charts
text: Customize radar grid shape divisions:
code: RadarChart(   height = 350,   series = list(     list(       label = "A New Hope",       data = c(film1$male, film1$female, film1$hermaphroditic, film1$none)     ),     list(       label = "Attack of the Clones",       data = c(film2$male, film2$female, film2$hermaphroditic, film2$none)     ),     list(       label = "Return of the Jedi",       data = c(film3$male, film3$female, film3$hermaphroditic, film3$none)     )   ),   shape = "circular", # or "sharp"   divisions = 3,   radar = list(     metrics = c("male", "female", "hermaphroditic", "none")   ) )

title: Radar Charts
text: Control highlighting highlight prop:
code: RadarChart(   height = 350,   series = list(     list(       label = "A New Hope",       data = c(film1$male, film1$female, film1$hermaphroditic, film1$none)     ),     list(       label = "Attack of the Clones",       data = c(film2$male, film2$female, film2$hermaphroditic, film2$none)     ),     list(       label = "Return of the Jedi",       data = c(film3$male, film3$female, film3$hermaphroditic, film3$none)     )   ),   highlight = "series",   radar = list(     metrics = c("male", "female", "hermaphroditic", "none")   ) )

title: Radar Charts
text: Customize tooltip behavior trigger option:
code: RadarChart(   height = 350,   series = list(     list(       label = "A New Hope",       data = c(film1$male, film1$female, film1$hermaphroditic, film1$none)     ),     list(       label = "Attack of the Clones",       data = c(film2$male, film2$female, film2$hermaphroditic, film2$none)     )   ),   radar = list(     metrics = c("male", "female", "hermaphroditic", "none")   ),   slotProps = list(     tooltip = list(       trigger = "axis" # or "item"     )   ) )

title: Radar Charts
text: RadarDataProvider dedicated data provider radar charts. Use RadarGrid, RadarAxis, RadarSeriesPlot, RadarMetricLabels build fully custom radar chart:
code: RadarDataProvider(   height = 350,   series = list(     list(       label = "A New Hope",       data = c(film1$male, film1$female, film1$hermaphroditic, film1$none)     ),     list(       label = "Attack of the Clones",       data = c(film2$male, film2$female, film2$hermaphroditic, film2$none)     )   ),   radar = list(     metrics = c("male", "female", "hermaphroditic", "none")   ),   ChartsLegend(),   ChartsTooltip(),   ChartsSurface(     RadarGrid(),     RadarSeriesPlot(),     RadarAxis(),     RadarMetricLabels()   ) )

title: Radar Charts
text: FocusedRadarMark renders visual indicator radar data point receives keyboard focus. Add inside ChartsSurface:
code: RadarDataProvider(   height = 350,   series = list(     list(       label = "A New Hope",       data = c(film1$male, film1$female, film1$hermaphroditic, film1$none)     ),     list(       label = "Attack of the Clones",       data = c(film2$male, film2$female, film2$hermaphroditic, film2$none)     )   ),   radar = list(     metrics = c("male", "female", "hermaphroditic", "none")   ),   ChartsTooltip(),   ChartsSurface(     RadarGrid(),     RadarSeriesPlot(),     RadarSeriesMarks(),     FocusedRadarMark(),     RadarAxis(),     RadarMetricLabels()   ) )

title: Radar Charts
text: RadarAxisHighlight renders highlight indicator hovering radar axis. Add inside ChartsSurface composition API:
code: RadarDataProvider(   height = 350,   series = list(     list(       label = "A New Hope",       data = c(film1$male, film1$female, film1$hermaphroditic, film1$none)     ),     list(       label = "Attack of the Clones",       data = c(film2$male, film2$female, film2$hermaphroditic, film2$none)     )   ),   radar = list(     metrics = c("male", "female", "hermaphroditic", "none")   ),   ChartsTooltip(),   ChartsSurface(     RadarGrid(),     RadarSeriesPlot(),     RadarAxisHighlight(),     RadarAxis(),     RadarMetricLabels()   ) )

title: Radar Charts
text: RadarSeriesArea renders filled area radar series. Use alongside RadarSeriesPlot filled-area radar charts:
code: RadarDataProvider(   height = 350,   series = list(     list(       label = "A New Hope",       data = c(film1$male, film1$female, film1$hermaphroditic, film1$none)     ),     list(       label = "Attack of the Clones",       data = c(film2$male, film2$female, film2$hermaphroditic, film2$none)     )   ),   radar = list(     metrics = c("male", "female", "hermaphroditic", "none")   ),   ChartsLegend(),   ChartsTooltip(),   ChartsSurface(     RadarGrid(),     RadarSeriesArea(),     RadarSeriesPlot(),     RadarAxis(),     RadarMetricLabels()   ) )

title: Scatter Charts
text: Scatter chart series contain data property array objects. object requires x y properties, optional id property.
code: # Prepare starwars data with two variables starwars_scatter <- starwars |>   filter(!is.na(height), !is.na(mass), mass < 200) |>   select(name, height, mass, species) |>   mutate(     id = row_number(),     species_group = case_when(       species == "Human" ~ "Human",       species == "Droid" ~ "Droid",       TRUE ~ "Other"     )   )  # Create two series based on species group series_a <- starwars_scatter |>   filter(species_group == "Human")  series_b <- starwars_scatter |>   filter(species_group == "Droid")  ScatterChart(   height = 350,   series = list(     list(       label = "Human",       data = lapply(1:nrow(series_a), function(i) {         list(           id = series_a$id[i],           x = series_a$height[i],           y = series_a$mass[i]         )       })     ),     list(       label = "Droid",       data = lapply(1:nrow(series_b), function(i) {         list(           id = series_b$id[i],           x = series_b$height[i],           y = series_b$mass[i]         )       })     )   ),   xAxis = list(     list(label = "Height (cm)")   ),   yAxis = list(     list(label = "Mass (kg)")   ) )

title: Scatter Charts
text: can use dataset prop datasetKeys specify columns use x y values:
code: ScatterChart(   height = 350,   dataset = starwars_scatter,   series = list(     list(       datasetKeys = list(x = "height", y = "mass"),       label = "Characters"     )   ),   xAxis = list(     list(label = "Height (cm)")   ),   yAxis = list(     list(label = "Mass (kg)")   ) )

title: Scatter Charts
text: chart computes Voronoi cells map pointer position closest element. can define maximum radius voronoiMaxRadius: Set voronoiMaxRadius = "item" trigger interactions hovering exactly element:
code: ScatterChart(   height = 350,   dataset = starwars_scatter,   series = list(     list(       datasetKeys = list(x = "height", y = "mass"),       label = "Characters"     )   ),   voronoiMaxRadius = 50,   xAxis = list(     list(label = "Height (cm)")   ),   yAxis = list(     list(label = "Mass (kg)")   ) ) ScatterChart(   height = 350,   dataset = starwars_scatter,   series = list(     list(       datasetKeys = list(x = "height", y = "mass"),       label = "Characters"     )   ),   voronoiMaxRadius = "item",   xAxis = list(     list(label = "Height (cm)")   ),   yAxis = list(     list(label = "Mass (kg)")   ) )

title: Scatter Charts
text: Customize size points markerSize prop:
code: ScatterChart(   height = 350,   series = list(     list(       label = "Human",       data = lapply(1:nrow(series_a), function(i) {         list(           id = series_a$id[i],           x = series_a$height[i],           y = series_a$mass[i]         )       }),       markerSize = 10     ),     list(       label = "Droid",       data = lapply(1:nrow(series_b), function(i) {         list(           id = series_b$id[i],           x = series_b$height[i],           y = series_b$mass[i]         )       }),       markerSize = 5     )   ),   xAxis = list(     list(label = "Height (cm)")   ),   yAxis = list(     list(label = "Mass (kg)")   ) )

title: Scatter Charts
text: can use multiple y-axes different series:
code: # Prepare data with different scales starwars_multi <- starwars |>   filter(!is.na(height), !is.na(mass), !is.na(birth_year), mass < 200) |>   select(height, mass, birth_year) |>   mutate(id = row_number())  ScatterChart(   height = 350,   dataset = starwars_multi,   series = list(     list(       datasetKeys = list(x = "height", y = "mass"),       label = "Mass (kg)",       yAxisId = "leftAxis"     ),     list(       datasetKeys = list(x = "height", y = "birth_year"),       label = "Birth Year (BBY)",       yAxisId = "rightAxis"     )   ),   xAxis = list(     list(label = "Height (cm)")   ),   yAxis = list(     list(id = "leftAxis", label = "Mass (kg)"),     list(id = "rightAxis", label = "Birth Year (BBY)")   ) )

title: Scatter Charts
text: ScatterMarker individual marker element rendered data point. Use inside ChartsSurface via composition API customise marker appearance slotProps:
code: ChartDataProvider(   dataset = starwars_scatter,   height = 350,   series = list(     list(       datasetKeys = list(x = "height", y = "mass"),       label = "Characters"     )   ),   xAxis = list(list(label = "Height (cm)")),   yAxis = list(list(label = "Mass (kg)")),   ChartsTooltip(trigger = "item"),   ChartsSurface(     ScatterPlot(       slots = list(scatter = JS("         React.forwardRef(function CustomMarker(props, ref) {           return React.createElement(             MuiXCharts.ScatterMarker,             Object.assign({}, props, { ref: ref, shape: 'cross' })           );         })       "))     ),     ChartsXAxis(),     ChartsYAxis()   ) )

title: Scatter Charts
text: FocusedScatterMark renders visual indicator scatter point receives keyboard focus. Add inside ChartsSurface composition API:
code: ChartDataProvider(   dataset = starwars_scatter,   height = 350,   series = list(     list(       datasetKeys = list(x = "height", y = "mass"),       label = "Characters"     )   ),   xAxis = list(list(label = "Height (cm)")),   yAxis = list(list(label = "Mass (kg)")),   ChartsTooltip(trigger = "item"),   ChartsSurface(     ScatterPlot(),     FocusedScatterMark(),     ChartsXAxis(),     ChartsYAxis()   ) )
